title: 1.1: Tests that pathFromBinaryLine returns an empty string when parsing a binary diff line where the destination path is /dev/null (file deletion scenario)

1.2: Tests that pathFromBinaryLine returns the repo-relative destination path (with the leading b/ removed) when parsing a binary diff line of the form Binary files /dev/null and b/<path> differ (file addition scenario)

1.3: Tests that pathFromBinaryLine does not trim or normalize whitespace in the extracted path when parsing a binary diff line containing a filename with spaces (i.e., the returned path preserves the exact spacing)
1.4: Tests that pathFromBinaryLine decodes git-style backslash-octal escapes (\NNN sequences representing bytes) in the destination path into the correct Unicode string in the extracted path
1.5: Tests that pathFromBinaryLine handles quoted destination paths in binary diff lines (i.e., strips surrounding double quotes from "b/<path>") before returning the extracted repo-relative path

1.6: Tests a quoted destination path where the first character after the b/ prefix is represented via a backslash-octal escape (\NNN), asserting the extracted repo-relative path begins with the decoded Unicode character(s)

1.7: Tests a quoted destination path that includes backslash-octal escapes and whose decoded final path segment (filename after the last /) contains at least one literal space, asserting the returned path preserves that space in the filename
2.1: Places the binary path parsing test in a Go _test.go file under pkg/gitparse/ (so it can call pathFromBinaryLine)
3.1: The added binary path parsing test is a Go unit test function with signature func TestXxx(t *testing.T) using Go’s standard testing package
